name: Villalbando and Camino de Santiago Loop
name_original: Villalpando — circular por el Busque de Los Campos – Camino de Santiago del Sureste
description: Hike the moderate 9.7-mile Villalbando and Camino de Santiago Loop, exploring nature and historic sites like Villalpando's Plaza Mayor.
region_intro: Hiking around Campos-Pan reveals a landscape characterized by natural reserves, river valleys, and reservoirs. The terrain includes flat paths around lagoons, gentle ascents along riverbanks, and some forested areas. This area offers a mix of wetland environments and open countryside, with routes often featuring water bodies and local historical sites. Best hiking trails around Campos-Pan The most popular hiking route is Laguna Salina Grande — return via the Villafáfila Lagoons Natural Reserve, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Villafáfila Lagoons Natural Reserve. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Perones and Cadozos waterfall — circular from Ricobayo de Alba, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.8 km) path. This trail features a waterfall and offers a circular journey from Ricobayo de Alba. Local hikers also love the Zamora city tour — circular from the sports city by the Castle and the Walls of Zamora, a 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail leading through urban and historical landmarks, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Hiking around Campos-Pan is defined by lagoons, riverbanks, and forested areas, offering diverse scenery. The network provides options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Campos-Pan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from nearly 20 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Campos-Pan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Campos-Pan?
A: Hiking around Campos-Pan offers a diverse landscape characterized by natural reserves, river valleys, and reservoirs. You'll find flat paths around lagoons, gentle ascents along riverbanks, and some forested areas. The region is known for its wetland environments and open countryside, with many routes featuring water bodies and local historical sites.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Campos-Pan?
A: Yes, Campos-Pan offers a good selection of easy trails. Approximately half of the 46 available routes are rated as easy. A great option for families or beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022943980/zamora-city-tour-circular-from-the-sports-city-by-the-castle-and-the-walls-of-zamora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora city tour — circular from the sports city by the Castle and the Walls of Zamora&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.9-mile (4.6 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 15 minutes and explores urban and historical landmarks.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in the Campos-Pan area?
A: Yes, there is at least one notable trail featuring a waterfall. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026677750/route-of-the-perones-and-cadozos-waterfall-circular-from-ricobayo-de-alba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Perones and Cadozos waterfall — circular from Ricobayo de Alba&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 4.8-mile (7.8 km) path offers a scenic circular journey and includes a beautiful waterfall.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Campos-Pan?
A: Many of the trails in Campos-Pan are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123961002/acenas-de-olivares-from-playa-de-los-pelambres-circular-by-the-rio-duero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aceñas de Olivares from Playa de los Pelambres – circular by the Río Duero&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4-mile (6.5 km) loop along the river. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022932221/bosque-de-valorio-y-acenas-de-olivares-circular-in-zamora-via-the-puente-de-piedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de Valorio y Aceñas de Olivares — circular in Zamora via the Puente de Piedra&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.5-mile (7.3 km) route.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: The hiking trails in Campos-Pan often feature significant natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Zamora&lt;/a&gt;, walk across the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3526335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora Stone Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5258374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora Cathedral (Catedral de San Salvador)&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its lagoons, such as those found in the Villafáfila Lagoons Natural Reserve.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Campos-Pan?
A: The trails in Campos-Pan are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from nearly 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, including lagoons, riverbanks, and forested areas, and appreciate the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Campos-Pan?
A: While the majority of trails are easy to moderate, Campos-Pan does offer a few challenging options for experienced hikers. There are 2 difficult routes among the 46 available. One such demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123665143/puente-de-valdelasmozas-from-el-campillo-circular-via-almendra-and-andavias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Valdelasmozas from El Campillo – circular via Almendra and Andavías&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 15.7 miles (25.3 km) with significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Campos-Pan?
A: Based on the region's characteristics, which include dry winters and wet summers in similar 'Campos' ecoregions, the shoulder seasons (spring and autumn) are generally ideal for hiking in Campos-Pan. These times offer pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather, making for more comfortable exploration of the wetlands, riverbanks, and historical sites.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the Villafáfila Lagoons Natural Reserve?
A: Yes, the Villafáfila Lagoons Natural Reserve is a prominent feature in the region. You can explore it on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025222045/laguna-salina-grande-return-via-the-villafafila-lagoons-natural-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Salina Grande — return via the Villafáfila Lagoons Natural Reserve&lt;/a&gt; trail. This moderate 6.3-mile (10.2 km) route takes about 2 hours 34 minutes and offers a great way to experience the reserve's unique wetland environment.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in total for Campos-Pan?
A: There are 46 hiking routes available in the Campos-Pan area. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 23 easy, 21 moderate, and 2 difficult trails to choose from.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, several trails incorporate historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026385091/castle-of-castrotorafe-round-trip-via-the-silver-way-from-fontanillas-de-castro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Castrotorafe - round trip via the Silver Way from Fontanillas de Castro&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.8-mile (6 km) route that leads to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrotorafe Castle (Ruins)&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022943980/zamora-city-tour-circular-from-the-sports-city-by-the-castle-and-the-walls-of-zamora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora city tour&lt;/a&gt; also passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Zamora&lt;/a&gt; and its ancient walls.
tips: A nice nature walk in the Los Campos area, especially the shade of the pine trees, is welcome in the vast open landscape that is typical of this area.

Like most towns in the Spanish-speaking world, Plaza Mayor is central to communal activities and Villalpando's Plaza Mayor is no different. Wide open space, sometimes used for festivals and bullfights.

Welcoming travelers from Villalpando since the 11th century, the Puerta de San Andrés is located on the southeast route to Valladolid. The arched gate is flanked by two defensive round towers on each side.

faqs.question: Is the Villalbando and Camino de Santiago Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally considered of medium difficulty and is suitable for most hikers, including beginners. It features mostly gentle gradients and manageable distances, making it a comfortable experience for those in reasonable physical condition.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characterized by mostly flat ground and moderate slopes. You will traverse through picturesque landscapes of thick forests and wide meadows, often following a mix of dirt tracks and asphalt roads. The route also crosses rivers via medieval bridges and passes through traditional Galician villages.

faqs.question: What notable features or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can experience the tranquility of the Los Campos Nature Walk. In Villalpando, you'll find the historic Plaza Mayor of Villalpando and the ancient San Andrés Gate, Villalpando. Further along, you might encounter historical landmarks like the Torre de Andrade and the medieval Church of St. James (Igrexa de Santiago) in Baamonde, as well as various medieval bridges.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The route offers a peaceful experience ideal for connecting with nature. Spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike, offering pleasant temperatures and lush scenery without the intense heat of summer or the potential for colder, wetter conditions in winter.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. Notably, it follows parts of the Camino de Santiago del Sureste (41% of the route) and the Camino de los Gallegos (29%). You'll also find smaller sections of the Cañada del Tesoro and Camino de Toro.

faqs.question: Is the Villalbando and Camino de Santiago Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally welcoming to dogs, it's important to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through villages, near livestock, or on sections that run alongside roads. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Villalbando and Camino de Santiago Loop. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for a 'Villalbando and Camino de Santiago Loop' is not widely detailed, as it's likely a conceptual loop around a section of the Camino del Norte. However, if starting in Vilalba (Villalbando), you can typically find parking in or near the town center, which serves as a common starting point for pilgrims on the Camino del Norte. Look for public parking areas within the town.

faqs.question: Are there cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Vilalba (Villalbando) and Baamonde, which are key points along this section of the Camino del Norte, offer basic services for hikers and pilgrims. You can find hostels, cafes, and grocery stores in these towns to resupply and rest.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and mixed terrain, pack comfortable hiking shoes suitable for dirt tracks and some asphalt. Bring sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing to adapt to changing weather. A basic first-aid kit is also advisable.

faqs.question: Is there a specific strategy for hiking this loop, such as a recommended direction?
faqs.answer: While the concept of a 'loop' is user-defined, if you are hiking a section of the Camino del Norte through Vilalba, the path is generally well-marked for pilgrims. Starting in Vilalba and heading towards Baamonde (or vice-versa) is a common approach. The first half of the Camino del Norte from Vilalba is known for being particularly quiet and scenic, which might influence your preferred direction for enjoying nature.
